insight: Gauss Cannon is considered underpowered to the point that spamming them does a poor job of countering Tatsu Nullifier, suggesting Gauss Cannon needs a buff (proposals ranged from 6HP to 10HP) to be competitive.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about buffing Gauss Cannon HP to make it viable, with masn6811 testing that 10HP Gauss Cannon is roughly the threshold needed to beat a fast Animus opening.

insight: Thorium Dynamo combined with other BP enablers (e.g., Trinity, Magna) creates oppressively BP-centric event sets; masn6811 proposed nerfing Thorium Dynamo to cost 6 (Elyot instead set it to 7), and both masn6811 and awaclus agreed 6 would have been the better number.
author: consensus
context: Post-patch discussion about the event mode meta being dominated by BP strategies due to Thorium Dynamo's presence in many sets.

insight: Pixie has a very low buy rate and minimal impact on most sets; expert consensus (masn6811, awaclus, silentslayers, mrguy888) is that units with ~1% buy rates are bad for the game, and Pixie and ladder Gauss Charge are cited as the weakest offenders among trinket-type units, with awaclus advocating for their removal from the ladder pool specifically.
author: consensus
context: Extended debate about whether Pixie and other low-impact trinket units should be removed from the ladder random pool, with jeacaveo defending them and awaclus/masn6811/silentslayers arguing against them.

insight: When attacking into an opponent with only a Wall and no attackers, clicking a Sentinel into the Wall wastes damage with no upside; Sentinel should not be clicked into a wall unless there is a meaningful target such as an Engineer.
author: shadourow
context: shadourow critiquing a play where two Sentinels were clicked into a Wall, with awaclus agreeing you simply don't click Sentinels into walls.

insight: Double Tarsier is stronger than two Sentinels as an early attacker not only because it is 4GG cheaper but also because Tarsier is not limited by having only 3 stamina, making it more flexible over a longer game.
author: consensus
context: velizar_ and awaclus comparing the cost-efficiency and longevity of Tarsier vs Sentinel as attackers when both get absorbed by a Wall.

insight: When planning attack timing against an opponent with only blue tech, the optimal Tarsier sequencing is to attack for 0 then 4 then 4 (e.g., two slow Tarsiers followed by two normal Tarsiers), avoiding attacking for 1 or 2 into blue because those small hits are inefficient against blue-based defenses.
author: masn6811
context: megasupp posed a theoretical question about the most difficult attack sequence to defend against using hypothetical fast/slow/normal Tarsier variants off a single Animus.

insight: Cluster Bolt's self-damage mechanic can be turned into a strategic advantage by deliberately planning your defense to absorb the Cluster Bolt you buy for your opponent, effectively neutralizing the downside.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ reflecting on what they find enjoyable about Cluster Bolt sets, specifically the planning involved in absorbing the gifted bolt.

insight: A Cluster Bolt + Chrono Filter combination (3 units with Conduit) can create a near-autowin rush for P2; P1 can respond with an early Conduit or counter Cluster Bolt, but P2 can answer P1's Conduit with a Cluster Bolt all-in, making this a potentially problematic 3-unit combo.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ and zera777 analyzing a Cluster Bolt rush that P1 couldn't defend with Animus or Chrono Filter, with zera777 noting 2-unit autowins are considered bad for the game.

insight: Centurion was nerfed specifically because its buy rate was approaching 99% on sets it appeared in, which is considered as bad for the game as a 1% buy rate — both extremes indicate a poorly balanced unit.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 explaining the design philosophy behind unit buy rates while arguing that Pixie's ~1% buy rate justifies its removal from the ladder pool.

insight: Doomed Mech is commonly underestimated; beyond its attack value it provides soak, making it more defensively flexible than it first appears.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ making a brief aside that they personally underestimate Doomed Mech and that its soak value is part of what makes it good.

insight: The term 'backlining' likely originated with Prismata co-creators Will and Elyot, or was coined organically by early players from the existing concept of 'backline defense'.
author: apooche
context: masn6811 asked where the term 'backlining' came from; apooche and silentslayers offered their best guesses.

insight: Certain achievement-requiring set-ups that are impossibly costly to pursue in competitive play are easy to execute against the bot, indicating the bot does not apply sufficient pressure to punish slow/greedy win-condition setups.
author: consensus
context: siepu, awaclus, and velizar_ discussing a rare achievement that can be set up vs the bot but would result in losing a real competitive game due to the opponent conceding or winning first.
